human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1021 requirements for media-buying-operations-specific workflow handling, evidence and confidentiality expectations, strategy, rate, financial, advisory, editorial, legal, contractual, and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal media buying operations execution, incomplete inputs, vendor and handoff failures, missing completion evidence, confidentiality conflicts, delivery exceptions, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence facts, conflicting policies, realistic terminology, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed media buy workflow status, IO readiness, vendor confirmation, trafficking handoff, delivery exceptions, invoice-support, confidentiality handling, and completion evidence rather than media strategy, rate approval, legal, editorial, privacy, financial, contractual, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes IAB/4A's media-buy terms, IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB, VAST, and ads.txt standards, MRC measurement guidance, FTC online advertising guidance, and NAI self-regulatory privacy framework with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported booking, handoff, reconciliation, unauthorized disclosure, unreviewed strategy or rate approval, unresolved delivery discrepancy, and generic treatment of materially different ad channels, buy types, vendor, or platform workflows.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, channel, publisher, vendor, platform, buying system, privacy, finance, and authority prerequisites for specialized media buying operations execution.
Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, mutation safeguards, and non-regression validation.
